extraction.one_line_summary: MATTHEWS Michael won from a 23-rider breakaway after attacking at 50km to go, dropped BETTIOL on final climb with 500m remaining after Italian had bridged solo.
tactical_patterns: POGAČAR attacked with 180km remaining forcing VINGEGAARD to chase alone, revealing Jumbo's willingness to let UAE burn energy early.
MATTHEWS attacked 50km out from large breakaway then defended on final climb, showing early aggression beats late positioning in reduced groups.
MOHORIČ 15+ minutes down allowed into break, demonstrating GC teams now permit even dangerous TTers freedom when sufficiently behind overall.
BETTIOL bridged solo to MATTHEWS on final climb but lacked acceleration to finish, confirming diesel riders vulnerable in final 500m gradients.

rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: Won at 38 years old after early 50km attack and defensive climb, peak timing defies age decline narrative.
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: neutral: Probing attack at 180km out forced but unsuccessful, suggests testing Jumbo's cohesion rather than genuine GC move.
rider_signals.BETTIOL Alberto: negative: Made heroic solo bridge on climb but couldn't finish Matthews, confirms lack of explosive finish after extended efforts.
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: Isolated immediately after Pogačar attack and chased by broom wagon, BikeExchange domestique shortage exposed on transitional stages.
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: Struggled to hold Vingegaard/Pogačar wheel and needed YATES Adam's help, third place increasingly vulnerable on any selection.
rider_signals.MOHORIČ Matej: positive: Positioned as most dangerous GC threat in break despite 15min deficit, Bahrain's new freedom for stage hunting confirmed.
